title: The One with the Dozen Lasagnas

jokes.text: Ross starts singing The Odd Couple theme song
jokes.setup: Random burst into TV theme song
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of spontaneous singing
jokes.explanation: Mild character quirk moment but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Chandler joins in the singing
jokes.punchline: Someone else gets caught up in the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation gag - gets a chuckle but predictable

jokes.text: Monica & Phoebe join in the singing
jokes.punchline: Now everyone's doing it
jokes.explanation: Completes the escalation but formulaic

jokes.text: No, no, we're done. We're done, man.
jokes.setup: Group gets carried away with I Dream of Jeannie theme
jokes.punchline: Someone has to be the voice of reason
jokes.explanation: Good timing and delivery to cut off the escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Well, the meat's only every third layer. Maybe you could scrape.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous suggestion that missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Monica's perfectionist logic applied absurdly to an unsolvable problem

jokes.text: Yep. You could plunk me down in the middle of any woman's uterus...no compass...and I could find my way out like that.
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and confident claim about navigational skills in wombs
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that's uniquely Ross - nerdy overconfidence taken to ridiculous extremes. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: It says in some parts of the world people actually eat the placenta.
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping disturbing fact
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor, fits Ross's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: And we're done with the yogurt.
jokes.punchline: Immediate loss of appetite
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy - great setup/payoff pair with the placenta joke

jokes.text: What do you want me to do with a dozen lasagnas?
jokes.setup: Fighting with aunt about catering expectations
jokes.punchline: The absurd practical problem
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's title premise, functional but not hilarious on its own

jokes.text: You kiss Uncle Freddie with that mouth?
jokes.punchline: Monica's sassy comeback
jokes.explanation: Standard comeback format, gets job done but predictable

jokes.text: [IN HIGH-PITCHED VOICE] Hi, Daddy. Hello. How come you don't live with Mommy?
jokes.punchline: Innocent question hits Ross's painful reality
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe innocence creating uncomfortable truth moment. Well-crafted cringe comedy.

jokes.text: How come Mommy lives with that other lady?
jokes.punchline: Getting more specific and more painful
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke, ratcheting up the discomfort

jokes.text: What's a lesbian?
jokes.punchline: The most direct and uncomfortable question yet
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the escalating series. Maximum discomfort achieved with minimal words.

jokes.text: It's like 'poke a nose.' Ah. Poke a nose.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's helpful but silly mnemonic device
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay that fits Phoebe's helpful but quirky nature

jokes.text: [IN UNISON] Blah, blah, blah.
jokes.setup: Characters getting bored with someone's story
jokes.punchline: Everyone simultaneously expressing boredom
jokes.explanation: Classic Friends group dynamic moment - they're all on the same wavelength

jokes.text: Wasn't this supposed to be just a fling, huh? Shouldn't it be...flung by now?
jokes.punchline: Playing with the word 'fling' literally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good timing. 'Flung by now' is a memorable phrase.

jokes.text: I mean, when I'm with him, I'm just totally, totally...Nauseous. I'm physically nauseous.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing romantic feelings, building toward 'in love'
jokes.punchline: Unexpected physical revulsion instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that subverts romantic expectations perfectly

jokes.text: I could call Immigration.
jokes.setup: What to do about Paolo situation
jokes.punchline: Nuclear option suggestion
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's appropriately over-the-top for the situation

jokes.text: Okay, you're gonna have to stop that...forever.
jokes.setup: Someone being overly cute about babies
jokes.punchline: Extreme reaction to minor annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good delivery line - the 'forever' makes it funnier than just 'stop that'

jokes.text: Need a new table. You think?
jokes.punchline: Obvious understatement about clearly destroyed furniture
jokes.explanation: Standard visual comedy setup with decent deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Uh, that's our friend Tanya. Of course it's your friend Tanya.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about photo, assuming it's a celebrity
jokes.punchline: Ross's embarrassed realization and self-deprecating response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's assumptions and awkwardness

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm having enough trouble with the image of you and Susan together. But when you throw in Tanya...[GROANS]
jokes.punchline: Ross imagining unwanted threesome scenario
jokes.explanation: Great character-based misunderstanding that builds on Ross's discomfort with the situation

jokes.text: The sex of the baby, Ross. Oh. You know the sex of the baby?
jokes.punchline: Clarification that deflates Ross's panic
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the misdirection setup, classic sitcom misunderstanding resolution

jokes.text: Or isn't.
jokes.setup: Talking about looking down at baby to see gender
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the other possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed dark humor callback - two words that completely change the meaning

jokes.text: I believe this piece of furniture was fine...until your little breakfast adventure with Angela Delveccio.
jokes.setup: Arguing about who broke the table
jokes.punchline: Revealing Joey's sexual encounter broke it
jokes.explanation: Good revelation joke that explains the visual gag and fits the characters

jokes.text: The impressions you made in the butter...left little to the imagination.
jokes.punchline: Specific visual evidence of the encounter
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy that's both specific and suggestive. Great Chandler delivery line.

jokes.text: We bought a hibachi together, and then he ran off and got married...and things got pretty ugly.
jokes.punchline: Escalating to marriage-level drama over a small grill
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation - treating a hibachi purchase with the seriousness of a romantic relationship

jokes.text: I don't mean to sound unprofessional, but, yum.
jokes.punchline: Completely unprofessional objectification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but fairly predictable workplace comedy

jokes.text: Ooh, oops! Being naked.
jokes.punchline: Him immediately stripping without waiting for answer
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that fits Paolo's character but not especially clever

jokes.text: Or an uncle.
jokes.setup: Monica excited about being an aunt
jokes.punchline: Correcting herself on gender possibilities
jokes.explanation: Minor correction joke that shows Monica's attention to detail

jokes.text: I'm familiar with his work, yes.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if they know Paolo
jokes.punchline: Treating Paolo's romantic activities like professional work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - sophisticated wordplay that's both clever and character-specific. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, Boy Scouts could've camped under there.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific camping metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe line - innocent camping reference for something sexual. Perfectly character-specific and hilariously unexpected.

jokes.text: So what? Like people are gonna come in and think, 'Uh-oh, I'm outside again.'
jokes.setup: Chandler rejecting patio furniture for indoor use
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple logic about furniture location confusion
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic - simultaneously dumb and kind of sensible. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: I don't know. Birds just don't say: 'Hello, sit here. Eat something.'
jokes.punchline: Imagining what birds would say as hosts
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist humor imagining birds as dinner party hosts

jokes.text: Oh, so forget about the birds, but big red insects suggest fine dining?
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the logical inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the furniture debate with sound logical reasoning

jokes.text: Kip would've liked the birds.
jokes.punchline: Bringing up the ex-roommate to guilt Joey
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that doubles as emotional manipulation. Great character moment for Chandler.

jokes.text: I don't make them a lot...because I don't think it's fair to the other cookies.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's concern for cookie feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - treating inanimate objects with emotional consideration. Quintessential character moment.

jokes.text: Which proves that I never lie.
jokes.setup: Rachel agreeing the cookies are amazing
jokes.punchline: Using cookie quality as proof of honesty
jokes.explanation: Clever Phoebe logic linking completely unrelated things. Sets up the serious moment perfectly.

jokes.text: So how does this work? Gonna balance the plates on these little heads?
jokes.setup: Looking at their new ornate table
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about using decorative table
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about impractical furniture design

jokes.text: Who cares? We'll eat at the sink. Come on.
jokes.punchline: Chandler's willingness to accept complete impracticality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how much Chandler wants this table

jokes.text: Pig man. Yes, good. Okay. Oh, but he was my pig man.
jokes.punchline: Rachel's possessive attachment to 'her' pig
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's conflicted feelings

jokes.text: You're like... Like the anti-Paolo. My Catholic friend is right.
jokes.punchline: Joey's random religious reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moments for both - Chandler's analysis and Joey's unexpected wisdom

jokes.text: Okay, that is either, 'How could you?' Or 'Enormous breasts.'
jokes.setup: Watching Paolo and Rachel gesture outside
jokes.punchline: Two very different interpretations of hand gestures
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about interpreting gestures. Very quotable Chandler line.

jokes.text: Paolo, I really hate you for what you did to Rachel. But I still have five of these...so heat it at 375 until the cheese bubbles.
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical cooking advice despite emotional situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her practical nature overriding personal feelings. Great callback to the lasagna premise.

jokes.text: Ross, you're so great. Oh, ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Ross giving romantic advice about finding better guys
jokes.punchline: Rachel unknowingly friend-zoning him while he's hitting on her
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom irony - audience knows Ross's feelings but Rachel doesn't. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: [STAMMERING] Am I having a boy? You're having a boy. Aah! I'm having a boy. I'm having a boy.
jokes.punchline: Ross's overwhelming excitement after trying to avoid knowing
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running thread about not wanting to know the baby's gender

jokes.text: [IN UNISON] We already knew that.
jokes.setup: Ross's huge excitement about the revelation
jokes.punchline: Everyone else already knowing deflates his moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Ross's big moment - classic sitcom timing

jokes.text: Yes. And that would be a shutdown. [IN UNISON] Shutout!
jokes.punchline: Everyone correcting him in unison
jokes.explanation: Minor sports terminology joke with good group dynamic

jokes.text: You guys are always hanging out in my apartment. Come on, I'll only use my left hand, huh? Come on, wussies.
jokes.punchline: Joey's desperate attempts to keep them playing including handicapping himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's competitive nature and desire for company

jokes.text: One more game? Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Joey desperately wanting to keep playing
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately agreeing after saying he was leaving
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment showing the friends' dynamic and Chandler's weakness for games

tmdb_overview: Ross keeps his baby's gender secret; Monica distributes extra lasagnas to friends.
